LAW AND MOTION 302, RULING: THE MATTER WAS ARGUED. AT THE CONCLUSION OF ARGUMENT, THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS MODIFIED. PLAINTIFFS DUNG TON & THU TON'S MOTION TO COMPEL RESPONSES TO REQUEST FOR PRODUCTION OF DOCUMENTS, SET TWO, AND SPECIAL INTERROGATORIES, SET ONE IS GRANTED, IN PART. WITHIN 5 DAYS, MERCURY CASUALTY COMPANY, ERRONEOUSLY SUED AS MERCURY INSURANCE COMPANY, SHALL SERVE FURTHER RESPONSES TO SPECIAL INTERROGATORIES, SET ONE AND REQUEST FOR PRODUCTION OF DOCUMENTS, SET TWO AS TO PLAINTIFFS' PERSONAL CLAIMS ONLY. THERE IS CURRENTLY NO BASIS TO EXPAND THE LITIGATION TO ENCOMPASS THE DETAILS OF OTHER PARTIES' RELATIONSHIPS WITH MERCURY CASUALTY COMPANY. OBJECTIONS ARE PRESERVED. (PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER.) JUDGE: A. JAMES ROBERTSON; CLERK: GINA GONZALES; NOT REPORTED.
ORDER GRANTING EX PARTE APPLICATION TO CONTINUE TRIAL

Notice Of Motion To Compel Responses To Request For Production Of Documents, Set Two, And Special Interrogatories, Set One
Off Calendar Mar-11-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Gene Halavanau, Esq., Tel. 510.379.5801, for plaintiffs and moving party;
Ian Fraser-Thomson, Esq. of Cesari Werner and Moriarty, Tel. 415.391.1113 x14, for defendant.

RULING: The matter was argued. At the conclusion of argument, the court adopted its tentative ruling as modified. PLAINTIFFs DUNG TON & THU TON's Motion To Compel Responses To Request For Production Of Documents, Set Two, And Special Interrogatories, Set One is Granted, in part. Within 5 days, Mercury Casualty Company, erroneously sued as Mercury Insurance Company, shall serve further responses to Special Interrogatories, Set One and Request for Production of Documents, Set Two as to Plaintiffs' personal claims only. There is currently no basis to expand the litigation to encompass the details of other parties' relationships with Mercury Casualty Company. Objections are preserved. (Prevailing party to prepare a form of order.) Judge: A. James Robertson; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Not Reported.

Notice Of Motion To Compel Responses To Request For Production Of Documents, Set Two, And Special Interrogatories, Set One

IAN FRASER-THOMSON, ESQ., 415.391.1113 X23, FOR DEFNDANT MERCURY CASUALTY COMPANY.

RULING: The parties did not stipulate to the Pro Tem Judge. The matter was argued. Report and Recommendation prepared.
